title: China-to-Samoa Solar PV Inverter Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: EMC — IEC 62920 / IEC 61000 Evidence for Samoa PV Inverter Projects
rows.target_requirement: No Samoa-specific standalone EMC technical regulation for solar PV inverters has been identified. In practice, Samoa PV inverter EMC expectations are likely to be set through MCIL import documentation, OOTR / EPC electricity-project review, donor or owner procurement specifications, and IEC or Australia-New Zealand legacy references. Exporters should prepare IEC 62920 EMC type-test evidence for PV power conversion equipment and relevant IEC 61000-series harmonic, immunity, and emission reports for the exact inverter model and configuration submitted to EPC or a project owner.
target_standards: IEC 62920 — Photovoltaic power generating systems — EMC requirements and test methods for power conversion equipment
IEC 61000 series — Electromagnetic compatibility emission, immunity, and harmonic-current test methods and limits where specified
MCIL import and business regulatory context for Samoa
OOTR / EPC electricity project review practice
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese PV inverter EMC evidence is often issued against GB/T 37408-2021 and related domestic EMC test methods, with reports from CNAS-accredited Chinese laboratories. Although Chinese reports may use IEC-derived test methods, the certificate scope, pass/fail limits, grid voltage assumptions, and model-family coverage may not match IEC 62920 or IEC 61000 evidence expected by Samoa project owners, EPC, or financiers.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
NB/T 32004-2018 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Gap: Chinese GB/T 37408 EMC certificates should not be treated as automatically accepted Samoa evidence. Provide IEC 62920 and applicable IEC 61000 reports from an accredited laboratory, mapped to the exact inverter power rating, firmware, filters, and grid configuration. Because Samoa uses 230/400 V at 50 Hz while China uses 220/380 V at 50 Hz nominally, confirm with EPC or the project engineer whether harmonic or conducted-emission test conditions need Samoa-voltage confirmation.

mandatory_status.target: Project-specific and commonly mandatory through EPC, owner, donor, or financier technical requirements; no standalone Samoa PV inverter EMC certificate has been identified
mandatory_status.cn: Voluntary or project-driven for export; domestic GB/T EMC evidence is not automatically accepted as Samoa IEC evidence

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Do not rely on a China-only GB/T 37408 EMC certificate for Samoa project submission. Prepare IEC 62920 and applicable IEC 61000 EMC evidence for the supplied inverter configuration, and confirm whether Samoa's 230/400 V grid voltage requires additional harmonic or conducted-emission confirmation.

source.publisher: Ministry of Commerce, Industry and Labour, Samoa

rows.requirement: EPC Grid Connection — IEC 62116 / IEC 61727 Evidence and 50 Hz / 230 V / 400 V Settings
rows.target_requirement: Samoa electricity-system approvals are practically driven by the Office of the Regulator (OOTR) electricity oversight and Electric Power Corporation (EPC) utility connection review. Samoa does not appear to maintain a strong dedicated national standards body issuing PV inverter product certificates, so project specifications commonly lean on IEC evidence and Australia-New Zealand legacy practice. For grid-tied PV inverters, prepare IEC 62116 anti-islanding evidence, IEC 61727 utility-interface evidence, model-specific protection settings, and commissioning records for Samoa's 230/400 V, 50 Hz grid. The frequency matches China, but voltage differs from China's nominal 220/380 V.
target_standards: IEC 62116 — Utility-interconnected photovoltaic inverters — Test procedure of islanding prevention measures
IEC 61727 — Photovoltaic systems — Characteristics of the utility interface
Samoa Office of the Regulator electricity oversight and licence framework
Electric Power Corporation grid-connection and project technical review practice
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese grid-connection evidence commonly includes GB/T 19964-2024 for PV power-station connection, NB/T 32004 inverter technical specifications, GB/T 37408 inverter requirements, and domestic anti-islanding or protection test reports. These documents are based on Chinese grid assumptions and nominal 220/380 V, 50 Hz service under China's domestic framework. They do not automatically demonstrate compatibility with Samoa's 230/400 V, 50 Hz grid or EPC project acceptance criteria.
china_standards: GB/T 19964-2024 — 光伏发电站接入电力系统技术规定 (Technical Requirements for Connecting Photovoltaic Power Station to Power System)
NB/T 32004-2018 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Gap: China GB/T and NB/T grid certificates are not a substitute for Samoa project-level grid acceptance. Exporters should provide IEC 62116 and IEC 61727 reports for the exact model or model family, a settings file showing Samoa 230/400 V and 50 Hz protection thresholds, reconnection delays, frequency and voltage trip points, and commissioning tests witnessed or accepted by EPC. Do not claim voltage equivalence with China: only the 50 Hz frequency matches; Samoa nominal voltage differs.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory in practice for EPC grid connection and OOTR-regulated electricity projects; exact documentary requirements should be confirmed project by project
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese grid-connection evidence may satisfy China grid operator requirements but is not automatically accepted for Samoa EPC grid connection

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat Samoa as a project-review market: provide IEC 62116 and IEC 61727 evidence plus EPC-specific firmware settings for 230/400 V at 50 Hz. China GB/T 19964-2024, NB/T 32004, and GB/T 37408 evidence can support the technical file but should not be presented as automatically accepted Samoa grid-connection evidence.

rows.requirement: Samoa Project Conditions — Island Grid, Cyclone Exposure, Salt Mist, and Apia Sea Freight
rows.target_requirement: Samoa is a Pacific small-island developing state with high renewable-energy ambitions and project procurement that often expects equipment to withstand coastal humidity, salt mist, cyclone exposure, logistics delays, and constrained island-grid operation. Grid-tied inverters should be specified with tropical-temperature derating, corrosion protection, enclosure ratings appropriate for coastal installations, surge protection, spare-parts planning, and sea-freight lead-time assumptions through Apia. These are usually project and utility acceptance requirements rather than a single national PV inverter certificate.
target_standards: Project procurement specifications for Samoa renewable-energy and EPC grid-connection projects
IEC 60068 environmental test series — temperature, damp heat, salt mist, and mechanical stress test methods where specified
IEC 61643 surge protective device coordination where specified by project design
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese inverter datasheets and GB/T 37408 or NB/T 32004 reports may list operating temperature, humidity, ingress protection, and altitude ratings. They may not include Samoa-specific cyclone exposure, salt-mist corrosion class, tropical derating curves, surge-protection coordination, spare-parts availability, or sea-freight lead-time commitments needed by an island project owner or EPC utility review.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
NB/T 32004-2018 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Gap: domestic China evidence should be supplemented with Samoa project-environment evidence. Provide model-specific thermal derating curves at tropical ambient temperatures, enclosure and corrosion documentation for coastal salt-mist sites, surge-protection architecture, mounting and wind-loading assumptions coordinated with the project designer, and an Apia sea-freight spares plan. Where evidence is based only on standard factory datasheets, ask the project engineer whether additional IEC 60068 or corrosion testing is required.

mandatory_status.target: Project-specific; often mandatory through EPC, owner, donor, or utility procurement documents rather than a single Samoa national product standard
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese environmental ratings are useful technical inputs but do not automatically satisfy Samoa project-owner environmental and logistics requirements

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Add Samoa-specific environmental and logistics evidence to the inverter technical file: cyclone and coastal salt-mist assumptions, tropical derating, surge protection, and Apia sea-freight spares planning. Chinese GB/T or NB/T inverter reports do not by themselves close these project-level gaps.

rows.requirement: IEC 62109-1 / IEC 62109-2 Safety Evidence — Samoa PV Inverter Projects
rows.target_requirement: Samoa does not appear to operate a strong dedicated national standards body issuing PV inverter safety certificates. For grid-tied solar projects, safety acceptance is therefore likely to be handled through MCIL import context, OOTR electricity oversight, EPC grid-connection review, project-owner specifications, and financier requirements. Exporters should prepare IEC 62109-1 and IEC 62109-2 type-test certificates and reports for the exact inverter model or model family, with installation manuals, markings, enclosure ratings, tropical derating data, and protective-device coordination suitable for Samoa conditions.
target_standards: IEC 62109-1 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 1: General requirements
IEC 62109-2 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 2: Particular requirements for inverters
MCIL import and product documentation context
OOTR / EPC electricity project review practice
rows.china_equivalent: China-side safety evidence commonly includes GB/T 37408-2021 and NB/T 32004 reports, voluntary CQC or CGC certification, and manufacturer test reports. These are domestic China baselines and may support product due diligence, but they are not automatically equivalent to IEC 62109-1/-2 type-test evidence for Samoa projects, especially where donor, utility, or project-owner specifications call for IEC certificates.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
NB/T 32004-2018 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Gap: a China-only GB/T 37408 or NB/T 32004 safety file is not enough for a Samoa IEC-oriented project package. Obtain IEC 62109-1/-2 certificates and type-test reports from an accredited laboratory covering the supplied model, firmware, cooling method, enclosure, and accessories. Add Samoa-specific installation controls for heat, humidity, corrosion, surge exposure, and grid voltage settings because Samoa's nominal 230/400 V supply differs from China's 220/380 V nominal supply.

mandatory_status.target: Project-specific and mandatory where EPC, project-owner, donor, financier, or procurement documents require IEC 62109 evidence; no single Samoa PV inverter safety certificate has been identified
mandatory_status.cn: Voluntary — China GB/T, CQC, or CGC evidence can support domestic conformity but is not automatically accepted as IEC 62109 evidence for Samoa

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Build the Samoa safety file around IEC 62109-1/-2 evidence, not China-only GB/T or NB/T reports. Confirm with MCIL, OOTR, EPC, the project owner, and any financier whether the supplied model family and installation conditions are covered before shipment.

source.publisher: Ministry of Commerce, Industry and Labour, Samoa
